element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Simple course, much shorter than others, establishes trackball/directional pad mechanics
Obstacles introduced progressively as courses become more difficult
More enemies added as game progresses; designed to obstruct player
Track surfaces make marble control progressively more difficult
Fifth race oriented opposite direction to other courses; features polka-dot visual patterns

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a race within time limit
Bonus time awarded for race completion
Visual theme change between courses signals pacing shift
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty curve with escalating obstacles, enemies, and control challenges; time pressure accumulates across race sequence

difficulty_curve.early_game: Practice course (Race 1) establishes basic mechanics in safe, simplified environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Races 2-4 introduce obstacles, enemies, and difficult track surfaces with increasing density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Race 5 (Silly) inverts course orientation and increases visual complexity; Race 6 culminates difficulty spike
notable_spikes: Transition from Practice to Race 2: introduces obstacles and enemies simultaneously
Race 5 (Silly): course orientation reversal creates disorientation spike
Final race: cumulative difficulty from all introduced mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Practice course (Race 1) intentionally simplified and shorter to establish marble control before introducing threats
Each subsequent race adds more obstacles and enemies; track surfaces become progressively harder to control
Each course has distinct visual theme; Race 5 (Silly) uses polka-dot patterns and opposite orientation to break player expectation
With exception of first race, remaining time carries over between races; bonus time awarded per completion, but cumulative pressure mounts
